findings.problem: The SIGUSR1 reset path is not one-shot. `reset_fault_requested` stays latched until some later fail-safe consumes it, so a reset requested before any fault can automatically clear a future `FAIL_SAFE_LATCHED` state.
findings.evidence: new/user/main.cpp:143-153 sets `state.motion_intent.reset_fault_requested = true` and never clears it on non-fault paths. The only clear is in new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp:531-533 when `motion.consume_reset_request` is true, and new/code/runtime/motion_supervisor.cpp:314-332 accepts any true reset flag once the re-arm hold has elapsed. The required contract is an explicit post-fault re-arm trigger, and task 3.1 calls the SIGUSR1 binding a one-shot intent (openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/design.md:378-393; openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/tasks.md:43).
findings.recommendation: Make `reset_fault_requested` edge-triggered for the current fault episode only. Clear stale reset intent outside `FAIL_SAFE_LATCHED`, or record/reset it against a fault-generation token so a pre-fault SIGUSR1 cannot satisfy a later re-arm.

findings.problem: The bounded frame-limit budget is still owned by product runtime logic instead of the smoke harness, so Phase B automation remains embedded in `main.cpp` rather than being kept wrapper-owned.
findings.evidence: new/user/main.cpp:83-90 loads `LS2K_MAX_FRAMES` into runtime state, and new/user/main.cpp:348-349 calls `RequestControlledStop(..., "LS2K_MAX_FRAMES")` from the product loop itself. new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh:42-46 and 78-80 inject the same budget into the runtime. The chosen design says bounded frame limits should be harness-owned policy from `run_remote_smoke.sh`, not long-lived product choreography (openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/design.md:307-309,319-334; openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/tasks.md:44).
findings.recommendation: Move frame-budget ownership into `run_remote_smoke.sh` and keep `main.cpp` limited to stop-request and DISARMED-before-exit behavior. The wrapper should decide when the budget expires and then request/verify controlled stop externally.

findings.problem: The smoke wrapper still writes its default verification log to an unrelated change directory, so checkpoint-3 evidence is misfiled unless the caller overrides `VERIFY_LOG_PATH`.
findings.evidence: new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh:7 defaults `VERIFY_LOG` to `openspec/changes/fix-board-runtime-hardware-detection/verification/runtime-smoke.log`. Checkpoint 3 for this change requires its verifier artifacts under `openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/verification/checkpoint-3/...` and task 3.2 requires the wrapper/evidence bundle to record harness context for this motion-lifecycle change (openspec/changes/implement-phase-b-motion-lifecycle/tasks.md:44-46).
findings.recommendation: Change the default `VERIFY_LOG` path to this change's verification tree, or derive it from the active checkpoint so the harness context and smoke log land with the correct checkpoint evidence by default.

findings.problem: The `SMOKE_LOCAL_ONLY=1` path cannot report failure, because the local runtime exit status is discarded and the script exits 0 unconditionally.
findings.evidence: new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh:208-209 runs the local binary with `eval ... || true`, and new/user/run_remote_smoke.sh:214-216 always `exit 0` after `run_local`. That masks timeout/runtime failures for the supported local-only wrapper mode.
findings.recommendation: Capture the local runtime status, append logs, and return that status to the caller instead of forcing success in local-only mode.
